Spotlight: Useful and broadly consistent sprinter whose Listed/Group form includes a 2yo win at Royal Ascot; faced a stiffer task in the Commonwealth Cup back there last time; has earned top weight on handicap debut and looks one of the main players; wears first-time headgear.

Consistent sort who has Listed/Group form; couple of good efforts since switched to handicap level, close second at Windsor latest; campaigned over 6f since last July but recorded his sole win (debut) over 5f; very solid claims, belatedly returned to this distance.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Comfortably defied a big penalty at Hamliton dropped to 5f, achieving the unusual feat of completing a novice treble; never figured in Royal Ascot handicap since, having been ridden further back than usual and met some traffic issues; with that defeat best forgiven, he remains of interest.

Fairly useful on his day; good second over C&D last summer and made all at Chester this spring; has faced mostly difficult tasks otherwise this term but is the least solid contender on 2026 bare form; behind unexposed Westport at Royal Ascot.

Generally consistent sort whose penultimate effort can be excused; made all in Class 4 contest at Goodwood (5f, good) last time, taking record to 2-14; back up two grades but receives weight all round and could go well; creditable third in sole C&D attempt.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 52,000euros foal, 55,000gns yearling; fifth foal; Naval Crown half-brother to 1m AW winner Swan Band (RPR 84); dam 1m AW 2yo winner (83), half-sister to two 7f Group-race winners; stable 7-47 (15%) with 2yos here in the last five years; the betting could be informative on debut.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten just over 9l when last of seven on debut in warm Newmarket novice (5f, good to firm) and took a step forward with 4l third of six at Ayr (6f, soft) a fortnight ago; bred to do better still; possible player.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £100,000 yearling; by Blue Point; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to Group 2 winners Penitent (1m) and Supplicant (6f 2yo), out of 6f winner; likely to improve for this debut outing but market support would bring him into the picture.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 58,000gns yearling; by Dark Angel; second foal; dam 5f/5.4f winner (including 2yo; runner-up in Nunthorpe), from speedy family; he may need this first run.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Backed into 15-2 and kept on for 2l fourth of ten on recent debut at Salisbury (6f, good); open to improvement on this second start and he's a leading contender.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Always towards the rear on debut at Ascot (6f, good) in May then showed much brighter pace when third of six at Haydock (6f, soft), beaten about 4l; the going may not have been ideal last time and he could be an improver now back on a better surface.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 58,000gns yearling, £160,000 2yo breeze-ups; second foal; Mehmas half-brother to 6f winner Unique Journey (RPR 102); dam US 8.5f turf winner, half-sister to some useful sprinters; represents powerful connections and could have a big part to play on debut.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: He's finished last of six on both starts (5f/6f, good/soft), but got closer to the target at Goodwood most recently when denied a clear run; in good hands to progress and he might not be far away in a first-time hood.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Slowly away and stumbled badly mid-race when 25-1 for last month's debut at Salisbury (6f, soft), and in the circumstances he did very well to keep on for fifth of 11, beaten just over 8l; his trainer is in fine form and he could have a bright future; interesting.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Pulled hard when fourth of 13 at 15-2 on recent debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm); this 120,000gns breeze-up buy is a brother to useful sprinting 2yo Cheerupsleepyjean and he could take a sizeable step forward if settling better; the tongue-tie is retained.

RaceName: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £85,000 yearling; third foal; Mehmas half-brother to KSA 7f winner Gaasyah; dam sprint maiden (RPR 68), half-sister to winners Mister Manannan (5f/6f Listed) and Shermeen (US 8.5f stakes; dam of 6f 2yo Group 1 winner Sudirman), out of smart 6f-1m1f winner; already gelded; trainer is having a good year with his 2yos and he could be a contender on debut; wears cheekpieces.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Finished third in a bumper and in all her races over hurdles, from 2m to 2m3f; didn't genuinely threaten to win any of them but this is an ordinary race and cheekpieces are added.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won a Southwell bumper on good ground in May but was disappointing under a penalty at Plumpton; however, she's a past winner representing the Skelton team and this isn't a strong maiden hurdle.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has finished last in all her races, two bumpers and a maiden hurdle at Worcester; now tongue tied.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: The Cheltenham & South West Racing Club
Spotlight: Similar profile to Getmyfriend in that she won a good-ground bumper at Southwell before coming up short under a penalty; has raced freely so the new hood may help.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won a 1m4f heavy-ground maiden in Ireland before changing hands for 60,000gns; 16-1 when narrowly beaten over C&D three weeks ago (good) and the winner was completing a hat-trick.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Just minor promise in two good-ground bumpers at this track and readily opposed.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 66-1 chance when well behind Likewhatyousee here three weeks ago.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Low-level bumper form even when she was placed; went off 25-1 for hurdling debut here and was about 18l behind Likewhatyousee.

OwnerName: A Bit On The Side & Partner
Spotlight: Brocklesby winner who appeared to run well in the Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot most recently; no certainty to back up that form, having posted his worst effort in C&D Listed event (behind Bill The Bull) on penultimate outing.

Ran well in C&D Listed contest on penultimate start, reversing Brocklesby placings with A Bear Affair, then made all in Bath maiden (5.7f, good) to become Adam Kirby's first 2yo winner as a trainer; has progressive RPRs and looks one for the shortlist.
Showed promise with second of five in 5f maiden at Goodwood; the bare form is well below Listed standard but he's open to progress; whether he can go one better at this level is another matter.

The son of winners for his yard; has shown progressive form over 5f at Beverley, most recently going close in conditions race when a 50-1 shot despite scoring readily the time before; sire's good 2yo campaign featured a C&D Listed success; respected.

Came up well short in the Norfolk at Royal Ascot but that assignment was probably too much too soon; comfortably made a winning debut in C&D contest (good) eight days earlier and looks interesting back in this scenario with progress still possible.

Thrice-raced filly who finished last of 27 in the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot most recently, running poorly even allowing for a difficult task; however, the promise of her ready success over that C&D the time before is by no means forgotten.

Sole newcomer in the field when appearing to need the outing in the Woodcote at Epsom; neck second dropped to 5f on the turf course at Lingfield later in June; may improve further but this seems a stiff task.

Opened her account in AW contest; has appeared to run very well on the figures in two starts since, latest when 11th of 27 in the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot where she was the rank outsider; leading player provided that form is backed up.

Runner-up at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) last month then went one better in commanding style in first-time cheekpieces at Pontefract (1m, good); could have more to offer in the headgear; strong claims under 5lb penalty.

Runner-up here (7f, good to firm) in May then went very close at Redcar (1m, good); couldn't get into it after a slow start at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) last time but still ran pretty well and he might not be far away.

OwnerName: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam
Spotlight: Won over C&D (good to firm) last June and followed up at Ascot (7f, good); he's well handicapped on that form and is one to watch in the betting but he's struggled on his six starts this season.

Returned from a break with good third at Ayr (7f, good to firm) in May and he went very close here (7f, good to firm) last Friday; he's done all of his winning at 7f but can be in the mix if the return to 1m isn't an issue.
Returned to form when runner-up at Ffos Las (1m, good to soft) last month on her second start back from a break; also effective on good/good to firm ground and she's one to consider.

OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Progressive 4yo who made it four wins from his last six starts when scoring at Hamilton (8.3f, good to soft) last month; also effective on good/good to firm ground; a 3lb rise should not prevent another big run.

Sent off favourite and returned to form with a win at Redcar (1m, good) a fortnight ago; he did it a shade readily and can give another good account off this 6lb higher mark if backing up that latest performance.

Won at Newbury (1m, good) in May on third start for this yard and followed up when unpenalised at Yarmouth (1m, good) five days later; runner-up off this 6lb higher mark at Goodwood (1m1f, good to soft) last time, when outstayed, and he could play a leading role back down to 1m.

Soundly beaten at Redcar (7f, good) a fortnight ago, so his losing run continues, but he was in good heart previously and kept on well when making the frame at Ayr (7f, good to firm) and Carlisle (7f, good); this first crack at 1m is worth a go and he's not ruled out each-way.

RaceName: Sun Racing Free Tickets With Sun Club Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three runs after wind surgery and all the better for it; maiden winner in March and just over a length off the winner on handicap debut at Worcester 22 days ago (2m4f, good); solid.

RaceName: Sun Racing Free Tickets With Sun Club Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Low-grade winner on the AW who has shown some aptitude for hurdling, making the frame in run-of-the-mill 2m maidens at Hereford (good) and Huntingdon (good to firm); not obviously well treated for this handicap debut but should feature in a weak race.

RaceName: Sun Racing Free Tickets With Sun Club Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three Flat wins in 2023; 0-10 over hurdles but was only reeled in late on under James Robottom here three weeks ago (2m1f, good); clear claims off 1lb higher.

RaceName: Sun Racing Free Tickets With Sun Club Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 17 defeats include eight over hurdles and latest fourth in a 2m Huntingdon handicap was remote.

RaceName: Sun Racing Free Tickets With Sun Club Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner (1m2f/1m4f) who hasn't offered much at all over hurdles, including his handicap debut here last time when tried in a tongue-tie; went off 25-1 that day.

RaceName: Sun Racing Free Tickets With Sun Club Novices' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-24; went close in this new headgear combination here in May but he's back on the slippery slope and out of the weights this time.

Overcame apparent greenness in small-field maiden at Catterick (7f, good to soft) where he hung left and hampered the favourite on his way to prevailing by neck; open to progress but this is unquestionably a stiffer task under a penalty.

Both starts over 6f on slow ground, winning at Chepstow then fifth of six (not disgraced on bare figures) at Goodwood; gelded since; faces a harder assignment in this field and pedigree gives mixed messages as regards the longer trip.

100,000euros yearling, 25,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Blackbeard half-brother to French 10.5f-1m4f winner Winema (including Listed; RPR 105); dam unraced half-sister to 9.5f Flat/2m hurdle winner Charlus; yard is 0-7 with 2yos this term.

Twice-raced gelding who has registered RPRs in the 80s by finishing a close second at Nottingham (good) then 11th of 21 in the Coventry at Royal Ascot (good to firm), both over 6f; leading form contender back down in class and upped in trip.

65,000euros foal, 92,000gns yearling; by Acclamation; sibling to five winners, notably Tabarrak (7f/1m including 2yo/Listed; RPR 117); dam 6f 2yo scorer (99); one of several newcomers in the field; market should be instructive.

Has shown clear promise in a couple of 7f maidens on good ground, latest at Sandown when doing well to finish a good third to Gymbaazy having stumbled amid traffic issues; gets a 5lb pull with that rival and may turn the tables provided this pans out better.

Mehmas half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Kindergarten (RPR 90); dam unraced sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner/Melbourne Cup runner-up Tiger Moth, half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed scorer Coach House, out of 6f Group 3 winner; check the betting.

From a useful family that has served his connections well; showed signs of greenness on his way to finishing fourth of seven in 6f event on the turf course at Lingfield; open to improvement with the experience under his belt.

300,000gns yearling; Lope De Vega half-brother to four winners, mostly useful or smart, notably Berkshire Rocco (7f-2m including 2yo/Listed; RPR 117); dam unplaced 1m AW (52); represents significant connections; interesting debutant.

56,000euros foal, 70,000euros yearling; by Churchill; sibling to four winners, including two in Britain, notably Sollertia (7f 2yo; RPR 84); dam 1m/8.4f scorer (including Listed; 98); stable has a modest strike-rate with 2yos.
He's just 1-25 on turf but was runner-up over C&D (good to firm) last month and wasn't beaten far in a competitive Class 2 at favourite course Newcastle (5f, AW) last Friday; could be in the mix.
0-10 since arriving from France but twice finished runner-up in the spring (5f, good) and he was a close fifth in a tight finish at Hamilton (5f, good to firm) last week; might not be far away.
Close third at Windsor (5f, good to firm) last month and another good run when runner-up at Brighton (5.2f, good to firm) latest; contender.

Some good efforts for her new yard this year prior to snapping a losing sequence with a win at Thirsk (5f, good) last month; that may have given her confidence a boost and she's respected back up 4lb.

Well handicapped on the pick of last year's AW form and he may have needed his comeback run at Thirsk in May; however, this 9yo was well beaten and is without a win on turf since September 2021; others are more compelling.

It's possible the drop back to 5f will help but Chester brought out the best in him for Roger Varian and it's been the same story for his new yard this term; has to improve upon what he's been showing away from the Roodee.

Won at Haydock (7f, good to firm) last May and at Newcastle (5f, AW) in November but he's a more reliable proposition on AW, on which he's 7-19 as opposed to 1-15 on turf; not at the top of his game on grass of late.

RaceName: St. Austell Brewery Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Completed a hat-trick over hurdles last year from 2m5f (here) to 3m (all on good ground); however, she's been well below that level for current yard, over hurdles and fences.

RaceName: St. Austell Brewery Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a 2m3f handicap hurdle in April for Richard Bandey whom she left for £20,000; 13-8 for recent yard debut at Hexham and went down narrowly over a trip just shy of 3m; obvious contender here off an untouched mark.

RaceName: St. Austell Brewery Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Only win came in this race 12 months ago off 1lb lower and she returns on the back of a 5l defeat three weeks ago; should run well, provided she takes to the new blinkers.

RaceName: St. Austell Brewery Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Bled in her final two races for Dan Skelton and was 100-1 when beaten a distance on yard debut in a 2m handicap hurdle at Southwell (good), albeit after a year off; chance hinges on this longer trip making all the difference and her pedigree offers hope.

RaceName: St. Austell Brewery Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Best run over hurdles when third of six finishers on her handicap debut at Stratford (2m, good to firm) in May but only fourth of the six next time; the ability is there should a first-time visor (replacing cheekpieces) do something for her.

RaceName: St. Austell Brewery Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Third in a handicap hurdle here in May 2025; however, well beaten in two other handicaps on either side of a brief but successful stint in points; it's hard to make a compelling case but Sean Bowen has been booked.

RaceName: St. Austell Brewery Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Pulled up on chase debut latest and very opposable back over hurdles from 7lb out of the weights; cheekpieces are tried.

RaceName: Davies Insurance Solutions Gala Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Ran creditably, one place behind Persica, in Epsom Group 3 last time but is now worse off with that rival; has to overcome a 3lb penalty for narrow Listed win at Goodwood (1m2f, good) in May.

RaceName: Davies Insurance Solutions Gala Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: In great form since wearing cheekpieces, completing a handicap double at Meydan then running well in better races, latest at Royal Ascot (1m2f, good to firm; Listed) back on home soil; solid claims in the retained headgear.

RaceName: Davies Insurance Solutions Gala Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Went close when bidding for a second win in the Diomed at Epsom and ties in closely with Dividend on the form of his respectable effort at Royal Ascot later in June; C&D winner on Eclipse day in 2024; possibilities.

RaceName: Davies Insurance Solutions Gala Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Hindered by major traffic issues when behind two of today's rivals at Royal Ascot last time; losing run is mounting up but he's 1-1 over C&D, namely a win in the 2024 Brigadier Gerard Stakes; interesting back here.

RaceName: Davies Insurance Solutions Gala Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Produced a very smart handicap performance at Epsom (1m2f, good to soft) on Oaks day, registering an RPR of 121 and taking record to 4-8; leading contender provided that form is backed up returned to Listed level.

RaceName: Davies Insurance Solutions Gala Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Sole 3yo in this field and is open to further improvement; ran well behind Bow Echo in one of his 2yo races; good third in the Hampton Court at Royal Ascot (1m2f, good to firm; Group 3) on reappearance; adds interest.

Goodwood winner for Ralph Beckett last August off 5lb higher; not at the same level for his current yard but he overraced last time, when cracking late over further, and looks back on a competitive mark; stable also runs Military Air.

Progressed during a light campaign last season and has taken it up another notch the last twice; this looks a deeper race than the Lingfield (turf) handicap in which he was narrowly beaten five weeks ago, though, and he'll need more again.

Dual novice winner early last summer who's generally strugged in handicaps, including in two runs since being gelded; the drop back from 1m4f should help but this will largely come down to how he responds to first-time headgear.

Ex-French gelding who's struggled badly for this yard, all under Daniel Tudhope, who rides Prince Of The Seas today; handicapper easing off quickly but he's hard to recommend as he drops from 1m6f back in the hood (tongue-tie also refitted).

Two easy wins from the front in recent weeks, appreciating the step up to this sort of trip back on turf; due to be 4lb higher from tomorrow, after last week's Newbury demolition, and her claims are obvious for a yard bang among the winners.

OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing I and Partner
Spotlight: Three fair efforts since returned to turf; two Wolverhampton wins early in the year came off lower marks in blinkers and he'll need a career-best to take this stepping back up from 1m.

Both starts over 1m on Tapeta last autumn, improving from his debut to land a novice that's worked out well enough; longer trip should suit on pedigree now handicapping and the market will give some clues after eight months off.

Built on her debut Ascot fifth when winning a small-field Lingfield (turf) maiden against her own sex in May; this a deal tougher now handicapping but she showed the right attitude there and remains open to further improvement.
Goes particularly well at Southwell where he won again by 15l a month ago (2m, good), making all; he was granted a soft lead there and has gone up 9lb.

Recently sold out of the Twiston-Davies yard for £27,000 and has since had a wind operation; failed to fire in two previous chases but ran well in his final race over hurdles, beating all bar a talented and revitalised winner; he's of obvious interest.

OwnerName: Both Sides Of The Sea Syndicate
Spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in his final two races when last seen a year ago; however, he won his first two in this discipline (2m on good ground) and would have every chance if returning in good spirits.

Dual hurdle winner; proving consistent over fences and carries a penalty for Monday's 20l success at Stratford (2m3f, good to firm) where he was left clear at the last; looks sure to put up another bold show, receiving plenty of weight off his rivals.

RaceName: Coral Marathon (Listed Race) (Registered As The Esher Stakes)
Spotlight: Both wins have been at Newbury, in a maiden over 1m3f last April and a Group 3 over 1m5f (good to firm) last August; respectable sixth in the St Leger in September but he's been below that level in all four starts since, including when beating only Duke Of Oxford home over this C&D in May; drops back to 2m having contested the Gold Cup two weeks ago.

RaceName: Coral Marathon (Listed Race) (Registered As The Esher Stakes)
Spotlight: Runner-up in the Derby at Epsom last June and kept on well for third in the Irish Derby at the Curragh (also 1m4f on good) next time; showed he retains plenty of ability when a 2l third in C&D Group 3 in May (ahead of three of today's rivals), leaving the impression a stronger gallop would have suited; should come on for that first start in 11 months and looks the one to beat.

RaceName: Coral Marathon (Listed Race) (Registered As The Esher Stakes)
Spotlight: Six-time winner on the Flat over shorter trips than this (1m1f-1m4f), including at this track in 2023; beaten about 5l when fifth of seven in a Group 3 over this C&D in May (behind Lazy Griff and ahead of Furthur and Duke Of Oxford) but that was steadily run and he didn't fully prove his stamina; stable won this with Trueshan in 2024 but this 7yo has a bit to find to reverse placings with Lazy Griff.

RaceName: Coral Marathon (Listed Race) (Registered As The Esher Stakes)
Spotlight: 4yo filly whose two wins came last spring in a Southwell maiden (1m4f, AW) and Chester handicap (1m4f, good); struggled on soft ground in a 1m6f Listed race at Musselburgh this April and although she ran well when Listed fourth over 1m3f at Baden-Baden (good to soft) four weeks ago, she faded and didn't look to be crying out for this far; has a bit to find.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Regressive since returning from almost a year off and has struggled in a couple of runs back on turf (said to have been restless in the stalls last time); this is easier than he's used to, dropping back from 7f, but he has it to prove now.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Weighted to her best still, despite having run poorly in two starts back; the visor worn for last year's three wins returns today, which is an obvious plus point and could bring about an improved showing.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive as a 3yo (C&D winner) but regressed last year and has continued to slide this season; hit a bit of traffic when not beaten far in first-time cheekpieces (retained) latest but others look to have stronger claims.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Currently 2-4 over C&D (also finished fourth in this last year off 4lb lower); a disappointing effort last time will need forgiving but the return here will help, this time with 7lb being taken off by Jake Dickson.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden who was disappointing as favourite when last seen five weeks ago; maybe that was down to the first-time cheekpieces (removed today) and this mark is well within range on the evidence of his other two runs since a wind operation.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Solid comeback before chasing home an improver at Hamilton; took a backward step as favourite over C&D (hung right) last time and is now tried in another form of headgear; went the wrong last year and has to prove he isn't following the same pattern.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
OwnerName: Come On Over Syndicate & A H Yates Ltd
Spotlight: Found his level now and the return of the visor, worn for his nursery near-miss last autumn off 7lb higher, had a positive effect over C&D last Friday; will be 4lb higher from tomorrow and his claims are obvious.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Fair in three runs on Tapeta last backend (6f-7f) before being gelded; inclined to overrace on his turf/handicap debut (6f) back from a break a couple of months ago and still has a bit of potential for a yard that got on the board for the year last weekend.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Ended last season with a couple of poor efforts and has picked up where he left off; soft ground was likely behind his last effort six weeks ago but he's hard to recommend even then.

Useful Flat form in France; after winning a maiden hurdle for Willie Mullins he was essentially underwhelming under both codes and has got nothing going in three runs for Dan Skelton, including handicaps at the Cheltenham and Aintree festivals; in calmer waters here after a wind operation and disappointing should he not show a lot more.
Three hurdle wins at around 2m; seemed to need his run in May and then rallied to threaten an unexposed winner at Worcester (2m4f, good); that was a solid effort and he's only 1lb higher.

OwnerName: Mrs S D Pearson & Ollie Pears
Spotlight: Beat three rivals in this 12 months ago and has added further wins since then, on the Flat and over hurdles; only beaten a length in 1m4f handicap last time after a wind operation; not handicapped out of it by any means.

Once a sprinter on the Flat but has won twice over hurdles since joining current yard, a Hereford maiden and a C&D novice (both on good); hard to know what to make of his remote second in a handicap back here a month ago when the winner strolled home by some 26l.
Lightly raced; it was only a Class 5 he won at Worcester (2m, good) but that was quite straightforward in the new tongue-tie and he's open to considerable improvement; raised a fair 6lb.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Handicap
Spotlight: Won a handicap at Pontefract (1m, soft) in October off a mark 4lb lower than today's but he's been below that level in two starts this year, latest at Sandown (1m2f, good) three weeks ago; steps up in trip with stamina to prove.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Handicap
Spotlight: Progressed with each of her three qualifying runs and took another step forward when making a winning handicap debut at Ripon (1m2f, good; first-time cheekpieces) two weeks ago; now 4lb higher but this Sea The Stars filly should have more to offer.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Handicap
Spotlight: Showed ability on his first two starts last year and heavy ground can be blamed for a poor run at Goodwood in September; beat only one rival home on his handicap debut at Lingfield (1m2f, good; first start since being gelded) but that was his reappearance, and his pedigree suggests this longer trip could suit; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Handicap
Spotlight: Showed improved form in first-time blinkers when a good second at Salisbury (1m2f, good) in May and he's posted two respectable efforts since, albeit he didn't look to relish this the step up to this trip at Newmarket two weeks ago; first-time cheekpieces replace blinkers.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Handicap
Spotlight: Proved well suited by the step up to 1m4f when a 3l second of six at Thirsk (good) last month but he was below that level when fourth of five at Windsor (good to firm) 11 days ago; may yet do better.

Gained most recent win last August; below par in the latest of his two starts this term but is interesting on historical data, having defied a 2lb higher mark at Sandown on Eclipse day four years ago; drops in class.

Record is 2-10; gelded prior to success at Thirsk and respectable effort at York this term, both over 1m4f; still on career-high mark but ran well in sole 1m6f attempt and may be capable of another good run.

Record is only 1-10, the success in AW maiden; however, shaped well in C&D handicap (good) most recently, staying on for third place in his first attempt beyond 1m4f; possibilities off current mark kept to this scenario.
Well treated on 2023 form but hasn't raced on the Flat for over two years and recent hurdles efforts suggest he's not the same horse now; needs to prove he retains peak ability.

OwnerName: R Vaughan, R Bisiker, M Jones And S West
Spotlight: Creditable second in small field on penultimate start; successful over C&D (off 2lb lower) last July but wasn't in the same form returned to this scenario (behind two of today's rivals) in latest outing.
Ran creditably in sole Sandown attempt (four years ago) but is still seeking a first British Flat success away from Goodwood; modest third there last time in a race he'd won in 2022.

Low-mileage 4yo who has a largely solid record, including two AW wins under Billy Loughnane, since handicapping; good second in a couple of turf events this season, latest upped to 1m6f at Carlisle (good); his turn in this sphere looks near.

Five-race maiden who was hindered by traffic issues over C&D (behind Marnier) most recently; has encouraging form otherwise and is bred to do better still, being a half-sister to three winners (including Group 1) for her connections; shortlisted.

Modest on the Flat but has shown an aptitude for hurdling; runner up in both completed starts at Clonmel (19.5f, gd-yld) in May and Punchestown (23.5f, gd-yld) 26 days ago; in between those runs unseated early at Kilbeggan; big player.

Disappointed when favourite in a Navan bumper on debut and had come under pressure when slipping up in a Killarney maiden hurdle when last seen in May 2025 when trained by Gavin Cromwell; new yard now and likely capable of better but O'Keeffe prefers stablemate.
Promise when third in a Clonmel bumper in April but no impact twice since, including when falling on hurdles debut at Downpatrick on latest at 150-1.

Order Of St George gelding; dam well beaten in bumpers, half-sister to winners Smitty Bacall (bumper and 2m4f-3m2f hurdle/chase), Palm Sur (bumper) and Sullivan's Brow (2m hurdle); likely best watched on debut.

Placed on sole French start; modest form in first two Irish maiden hurdles on soft and heavy ground before pulling up on handicap debut at Fairyhouse (3m, yld-sft) 90 days ago; brother to a good-ground winner, so could improve for this sounder surface; can't discount.
Left previous modest efforts behind when an 11.5l fifth of 12 in a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle (2m, gd-yld) 18 days ago; a good bit more needed to take this.
Valirann gelding soundly beaten in first two point-to-points before pulling up on latest at Dromahane in April; hard to fancy and best watched on Rules' debut.

Point-to-point winner (yielding) has hit the frame in five of seven starts over hurdles and rated 119; leading claims on winter maiden hurdle form but well below that level the last twice and needs to get back on track.
Placed in both point-to-points and in three of four bumpers; decent 5.5l third of 15 on hurdles debut at Tramore (2m, good); dam a 2m5f winner so could improve for this longer trip; shortlisted.

Failed to trouble the judge in both starts in bumpers; 5.5l behind Captain Hanley when fourth of 17 on hurdles debut at Clonmel (19.5f, gd-yld) in May; sharper for that and could get closer to that rival now; preferred by Darragh O'Keeffe.
Triple-figure odds and soundly beaten in three 2m maiden hurdles on heavy ground in January/February; better ground now but can only be watched.
Best run in bumpers a 7.75l fifth of 9 at Downpatrick in May (2m2f, good); didn't back that up when beaten 36l back there on latest; best watched on hurdling debut.

Fine effort when a close second on Rules' debut in a Galway bumper last August and highly tried on two of next three starts to no avail; well beaten favourite on hurdles debut at Kilbeggan 49 days ago; needs more.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Progressed well once handicapping as a 4yo and kicked on again last season, landing a hat-trick in late summer, latterly here off 2lb higher; not at her best in the spring but this is her time of year and she offered more latest; respected.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won this last year off 7lb lower, when rank outsider in a small field; possible she didn't take to Ripon last time, after a career-best effort at Pontefract, but she's often done for early pace over this trip and needs to get it bang on off this mark.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Highly tried after winning a Newbury maiden last August; offered more on her second run back a month ago, dropped to this trip for the first time, and today's more demanding 5f should suit for a yard back among the winners in recent days.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Landed her third win of the year three weeks ago, showing the right attitude on very soft ground (made all in a small field); this is more competitive but she's only been nudged up 1lb and that confirmed her back in form.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Three poor efforts since returned to turf; now back to the mark off which she won at Yarmouth (sole turf win) last July but she'll need a major revival against some in-form sprinters.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent filly who's been inclined to pull hard over 6f here the last twice; did well to get as close last Friday, given she fluffed the start and had to switch for a run, and has a chance of breaking her duck on turf (0-13; three-time winner on Tapeta).

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: String of solid runs this year, aside from when not handling the Ripon undulations in May; again inclined to pull hard when beaten on the nod last time and is given a first go over 5f off the back of it (only 1lb higher); holds claims in an open handicap.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Career-best effort when winning at Thirsk last time; will need another forward step, upped 4lb into a stronger race, but that wouldn't come as a surprise.
Generally consistent sort gained a second handicap win at Down Royal (7f, soft) two weeks ago off 58; in form but most of best turf form is on softer ground so probably needs rain.
66-1 and tailed off in a 1m5f Down Royal maiden on debut 13 days ago; hard to fancy down in trip on the back of that tame effort.

OwnerName: Dooley Thoroughbreds & J Fairhurst & Deepak Dhand
Spotlight: Below best in handicaps on last two starts; solid form in claiming company (form figures of 3113); handles most ground and went close over C&D on her only previous visit in October; big player.

Maiden produced back-to-back solid runs at Ballinrobe and the Curragh earlier this season; below that level the last twice and drops into claiming company for the first time; yard does well in these races (39-332) so not discounted.

Maiden has run some decent races in defeat this season, including when a close second off 63 in a Down Royal handicap (7f, soft) two weeks ago; effective on good ground so dangerous to dismiss on first try in claiming company but needs to rebound from below par Fairyhouse run on Wednesday.
0-8 and rated 64; runner up on debut in a Galway Festival maiden last August but failed to trouble the judge since; no improvement for cheekpieces latest; blinkers tried now.

Rated 68 so well treated by race conditions on paper and one of best efforts for this yard when beaten 6.5l into seventh of 15 in Leopardstown handicap (7f, good) on seasonal return; needs to back that up now.

Locally trained; ran well over 7f at Down Royal on penultimate when a 1.75l fourth; midfield over an inadequate 6f at Fairyhouse last month; return to 1m a plus and last win came in a 1m Killarney claimer in 2024; fourth 6.10 here on Thursday.

OwnerName: Mark Devlin & Nigel O'Hare & Philip Smith & Bart O'Sullivan
Spotlight: Mostly running well this year; won at Ayr in May off 63 (1m, gd-fm) and not beaten far the last twice; 4l third of 10 in a 1m Dundalk claimer on penultimate and beaten 2.25l in Down Royal handicap latest; in the mix.

OwnerName: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney
Spotlight: Four-time winner; out of form recently and hasn't scored on turf since September 2023 but plenty of placed efforts to his name over this C&D so has to be respected on first run in claiming company with promising rider taking off 7lb; unplaced 6.10 here on Thursday.
Dual-purpose maiden; multiple placed efforts on the AW but 0-7 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; heavy defeat back on the Flat at Leopardstown on latest; others preferred.

Promise in AW maidens in Britain and on debut for this yard in February but failed to make any impact in two handicap runs on turf at Limerick (1m, yielding) and Fairyhouse (1m2f, good); can only be watched unless the market speaks.

Sole success came on the beach at Laytown last September; lost way since but minor step in the right direction when beaten 6l in midfield at Leopardstown last time; has run well in claiming company before; could outrun odds for local yard.
One of best efforts yet on latest when fifth at Fairyhouse (1m2f, good) but was still beaten 9l off a mark of 57; more needed; due to run 5.40 Fairyhouse on Wednesday.
No sign of winning potential in four maiden runs at 5f-8.5f on varying ground; can only be watched on return from 260 day absence.
Four-time chase winner but nothing positive to report over fences or hurdles in his last four races.

Ex-Irish pointer; benefited from the step up to this trip when winning by 8l here in early June and a slow pace back over hurdles found him out three weeks ago, though didn't run badly; 8lb higher than for both those runs but still has higher hopes than some in here.

Maiden under rules in Ireland (0-3 as a chaser) but he had a few wins and places to his name in 25 point starts; the latest wasn't his best but he still went and joined Dan Skelton for £8,000; that alone heightens interest in him.
Sole success came in a 3m handicap chase on good ground at Perth last August when 6lb lower; he can cope with this mark but needs a return to form on his stable debut.

OwnerName: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner
Spotlight: Had a few near misses before finally off the mark over 3m at Uttoxeter (good) where the front two came clear; as a last-time-out winner in this company he has to be of interest; up 4lb.
Good and bad in five runs for current yard, all of which were at Fontwell and Fakenham; soft ground was probably against him last time and he stays well.
In a new visor he won two C&D handicaps last August; hit and miss since then but the visor in which he has form figures of 115 now belatedly returns.

Only 1-16 over fences but it was his first run after wind surgery when coming from off the pace for a closing third at Bangor (3m, good) last week; that's something to build on and Sean Bowen is back.

RaceName: UB40 Ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins from 19 starts, the most recent success coming on the AW at Lingfield in April off a mark 2lb lower than today's; however, he was disappointing back on turf at Salisbury (1m4f, good) last month and needs to bounce back; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.

RaceName: UB40 Ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when winning at Southwell (1m4f, AW) in November and left the impression her first start in seven months was needed when fifth at Kempton (1m4f, AW) three weeks ago; unexposed on turf and she's considered.

RaceName: UB40 Ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Showed improved form when winning over this C&D in May and followed up at Leicester (1m2f, good) next time; raised 4lb for that latest success and she was held off this mark at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) two weeks ago; may be high enough in the weights now.

RaceName: UB40 Ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won a maiden at Ballinrobe in June 2023 when trained by Joseph O'Brien; well beaten over hurdles on her final two outings for David Pipe but she did run creditably on the Flat in February; market support would be interesting on her first start for another new stable.

RaceName: UB40 Ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt when narrowly coming out on top in a steadily run race at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm) last week; escapes a penalty for that success and is respected.
Ran poorly last time; generally consistent otherwise this year, over a variety of trips, and may rebound; however, hasn't won for nearly three years and concedes weight all round in this field.
Scored off current mark on reappearance (1m2f, AW; in February) but hasn't been in the same form since, latterly back on turf, disappointing favourite returned to 1m most recently.
Three-time AW scorer at Kempton; opened his turf account in apprentice event under Oliver Carmichael at Goodwood (1m, good to soft) most recently; may remain competitive, back up just 2lb.
Has broadly respectable form since returned to turf, notably a good effort behind Dovey Moon; however, still may be more interesting when back on Chelmsford AW; 2-2 there compared to 0-18 elsewhere.
Successful for two previous yards but holds weak claims on his two efforts for current stable; chance largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
Consistent this year and recorded a clearcut win at Lingfield (7f, good) returned to turf last time but everything went his way (made all against favoured near rail); no certainty to defy a 6lb rise.

Gelded prior to making all and turning over a 1-5 shot in novice event at Lingfield (7.5f, good) on stable/seasonal debut; may build on that win and improve further, having raced only three times; interesting handicap debutant.

OwnerName: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner
Spotlight: Ex-French 4yo who is 0-8 in Britain and not solid on 2026 form, with his second behind H Key Lails at Lingfield flanked by duck eggs; still has something to prove beyond 7f to boot.
Lightly raced gelding who hasn't progressed from his 5f 2yo success, latterly in two races at about 7f this season for new yard; enough to prove upped further in trip.

Thrice-raced filly whose promise over 7f features second-place finishes at Newmarket (behind a useful sort) and Lingfield (AW) this term; may progress now handicapping, provided she stays the extra furlong; respected off bottom weight.

OwnerName: K McNamee & Manorplace Ltd & V Casey & F G Doran
Spotlight: Hurdles win in 2024 came at Listowel (2m, soft) off 91; returned there last September for a staying-on third in the same event; pulled up at Cork in May on reappearance after a winter break; watching brief on this quicker ground.

Returned for promising handicap debut at Down Royal in early May, none too fluent and short of room at crucial time when third of 20 over today's trip (good); later in month disappointing favourite over C&D, jumping letting him down; remains a potential improver given more fluent round of jumping.

Bumper winner is 0-9 over hurdles, best form on soft; promising Kilbeggan chasing debut in May and fair third in Downpatrick handicap on final start for Gordon Elliott; headgear left off here on yard debut back hurdling.

Sole hurdles win came over a year ago at Clonmel (2m4f) off 111; tried several times in claiming hurdles this year, notably C&D second in May, reportedly found to have blood in nostrils last month when just fair fifth in Downpatrick handicap; has to be treated with some caution but down to a nice mark.

Sole hurdles win came in 2m3f Limerick handicap last May on good; claimed out of Declan Queally's yard at Clonmel in May (4,000euros) and solid start for new connections when staying-on third in 2m4f Clonmel handicap last month; one to consider.
Mare is now 0-8 hurdling, one of better efforts on latest when front-running fourth of 14 over C&D; more needed but possible.
Hasn't completed in two handicap attempts, last-flight faller (beaten) at Navan and pulled up after not handling track at Bellewstown; possibly best watched for now.
Placed twice back in 2024; comeback run in April wasn't devoid of promise but lot more needed.
Didn't land a blow on last month's handicap debut at Listowel but some promise; potential improver.
Both wins have been on decent ground (2m); absent since halfway faller at Navan in November 2024 so likely to need this comeback run.
Very ordinary form in maidens and no sign of progress so far in handicaps; first-time cheekpieces need to bring serious improvement.
Soundly beaten in both recent handicaps at big odds; hard to fancy.
Latest Kilbeggan run only mildly encouraging, lots more needed.
Flat winner is now 0-14 over hurdles, well held over C&D on penultimate, badly hampered at Tramore since.

Ran well late last year on first four starts and showed improved form with 33-1 handicap win on reappearance at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last Tuesday; strong claims under 6lb penalty if this doesn't come too soon.
Lightly raced 4yo who has more realistic claims now back in a handicap but all of her rivals are 3yos and it's likely she'll be vulnerable.

OwnerName: All About York And Whatton Manor Stud
Spotlight: Won Catterick novice (7f, good) in April on her second start; only seventh of ten on subsequent handicap debut but she was slowly away; only 4-1 that day and retains potential; could bounce back with a big performance.
Won Kempton maiden (7f, AW) in January on fourth start and she's run well to make the frame in her first two handicaps (7f, AW/good to firm); there's every chance that she'll be in the mix.

0-5 in France but displayed ability and she was returning from a three-month break when sixth of ten on recent stable debut at Carlisle (7f, good); could show the benefit of that outing today; not ruled out.

0-6 but has run well in fourth in 8.3f handicaps at Nottingham on her two runs this year, both on good ground in April, and she's shaped each time as though the drop back to 7f might help; possible player.

Beaten 6l when fifth of six on seasonal/handicap debut at Lingfield (7.6f, good to firm) in May but might have needed the run; showed promise on final 2yo outing and she's bred to be better than this mark; interesting.

Beaten on first ten starts but got off the mark at the 11th attempt at Ripon (6f, good) in May and followed up in big field over this C&D (good); clearly progressive and a 4lb rise for last time may not stop her.

Returned to form with a bang when easily making all at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last month; she has an in-and-out profile and is unproven on turf, while she's 9lb higher today, but she could take plenty of catching if reproducing that latest performance.

Lost her action when soundly beaten on handicap debut at Yarmouth in April; went beyond 6f for first time at Lingfield (1m, AW) last month when she failed to settle and weakened into fourth; the drop to 7f could suit.

Won in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) at Ripon (1m, good) in May; it's a concern she pulled hard when no match for the front two at Leicester (1m, good) last time but if settling better she could be a force.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 90,000euros yearling who was sent off the 3-1 favourite for his debut at the Curragh (6f, good) but weakened tamely into eighth; likely capable of better given the apparent confidence behind him first time out.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Not friendless in the betting at 5-1 when beaten 3.5l at Down Royal (7f, soft) and getting upset in the stalls didn't help matters; open to improvement.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: First foal out of a 7f/10.6f AW winner (RPR 80); stable has a good 2yo in their ranks so market support could be significant.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 15,000euros foal; half-brother to 5f winner Shoebox King (RPR 82); dam unplaced 6f-1m2f (71), half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Came From The Dark; stable has won this race twice since 2021; market informative.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Much improved second time out when runner-up at Down Royal (7f, soft) where Full Volume was back in sixth; that form sets the standard here.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Sir Thomas Gresham (Listed-placed 6f) and Agent Allison (Group-placed 6f 2yo); likely best watched unless betting suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Never in contention at Down Royal (7f, good) when behind Kraar and Full Volume.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Pretty much tailed off in his first two races and he's getting himself ready for handicaps.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series (C & G) Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 45,000gns breeze-up buy; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Claritudo (1m4f/1m6f; RPR 79) and See The Green (1m Flat, 77; 2m hurdle); dam Listed-placed 1m AW winner (94); he would command plenty of respect if at all strong in the betting.

Both wins have been over 6f, the most recent at Salisbury (good) in October off a 1lb lower mark than today's, but he went over today's trip at Goodwood (soft) last month; likely has quicker ground conditions today but has good claims.

Four wins from 14 starts, the most recent when dead-heating at Southwell (7f, AW) in October; struggled on his first three starts this year but didn't shape badly at Newbury (7f, good to soft; first start since wind surgery) three weeks ago; drops in grade with blinkers added.

The most recent of his six wins came at Lingfield (7f, AW) in April 2025 off a mark 3lb higher than today's but he has posted some good efforts since; however, he was below his best at Salisbury 19 days ago and needs to bounce back with cheekpieces back on.

Made a winning debut at Salisbury (7f, good to firm; hooded) last July and was placed in novice company on his next two starts; however, he failed to beat a rival home on his handicap debut in September and pulled to hard on his reappearance (first start since gelding) in May; hood returns and drops back in trip.

All three wins on turf came on testing ground in France and his best efforts in Britain have been on the AW, including a win at Kempton in October; below par in recent starts and has been dropped another 2lb.

Gained his third success of the season when winning at Salisbury (6f, soft) last month and he was a good second of ten at Windsor (6f, good to firm) six days ago; steps back up to 7f with solid claims.
Tramore maiden winner in 2024 has largely struggled in handicaps; absent since modest fourth in Tramore Flat maiden last August so likely best watched.
Useful Flat form and not much to show over hurdles until much improved for C&D win in late May under more positive ride, clear some way out; 9lb rise for that so could well have more to offer.
Won 2m1f Clonmel handicap in April (yielding), couple of ordinary runs since off revised mark, albeit hampered at same venue on latest; bit to prove now.
Maiden winner whose standout effort in handicaps when C&D second in May; didn't land a blow in better-class Listowel contest on latest but respected back in grade here.
Maiden has struggled off stiff mark in opening handicaps; down 4lb but remains of limited appeal unless first-time tongue-tie brings serious improvement.
Off the mark over C&D in May last year (good) and second in similar event subsequently off 99; recent Clonmel comeback run should have brought him on and one for the shortlist.
Lightly-raced 5yo mare was never sighted on handicap debut at Downpatrick; tongue-tie now tried.
Long-standing maiden over hurdles but numerous placings, including good second in 2m3f Punchestown lady riders' contest last month; has his chance again, encouraging jockey booking.
Three minor wins on the Flat; has struggled in maiden hurdles, but possible improver now switched to Gordon Elliott on handicap debut.

Veteran finally made the breakthrough in 2m1f Bellewstown handicap in April and duly followed up at Down Royal off 5lb higher; solid C&D run since off further 8lb higher mark when chasing home a clear-cut winner; contender again.
Unexposed 5yo entering handicaps on the back of three poor runs in maidens; hard to fancy off this mark but worth a market check.

Attracted attention of Stewards over C&D in May before much-improved showing when favourite at Sligo last month, every chance when second over 2m5f (good); back in trip here and has to be on the shortlist.
Placed over this trip at Bellewstown last summer (good) off 80, badly hampered when pulled up on comeback run at Listowel in late May so that run forgiven; blinkers now tried.
Best handicap run when fourth at Roscommon last year but well below that level twice after and dropped out quickly on seasonal return last month at Sligo; blinkers tried.

RaceName: Your Weekend Starts Here Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 1m3f win at Carlisle (good to firm) last summer; began his 2026 campaign with two solid efforts but his form dipped at Carlisle 24 days ago (Class 5; sent off favourite); has to enter calculations at this modest level.

RaceName: Your Weekend Starts Here Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 1m4f wins at Doncaster (good) and here (good to firm) for new yard this summer; he has come up short in stronger company on his last two starts but holds good claims back in a Class 6.

RaceName: Your Weekend Starts Here Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Did well on AW earlier in the year and he ran well back on turf when third of 12 over C&D (good to soft) in April; latest Chester run easy to excuse (Class 4) and this is far more suitable; respected, for all there is a slight question mark surrounding fast ground.

RaceName: Your Weekend Starts Here Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins in 2025, two on the AW (1m4f) and once over hurdles; banging at the door on turf this summer, second over C&D ten days ago when not enjoying a clear run; another good run seems likely.

RaceName: Your Weekend Starts Here Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two AW wins in France but little to celebrate in Britain; two AW runs last autumn were respectable but nothing comparable this year; the new headgear needs to prove transformative.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner on soft ground in April but that was off 6lb lower and he's struggled since.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner and 2lb beneath his last winning mark; beaten about 4l in his last two races at 1m1f so not in bad form and he might get a good pace to chase, which helps him.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
OwnerName: Charles Kerwin & Brendan Kerwin & Liam Kehoe
Spotlight: Keeping herself busy; came home strongly under Orla Tynan to win at Roscommon (7.5f, good) but went up 10lb for that and was only midfield at the Curragh, albeit over 6.5f that was probably too sharp; others might be better in.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time winner who is in a consistent run of form and he got himself too far back when fourth of 16 at Leopardstown two weeks ago (1m, good); hard to discount.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: A close third at Dundalk last time (1m AW) where she often runs well and has won four times; thoroughly exposed now and only 1-22 on the grass.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple winner; on a good mark but hasn't been all that threatening this year and others appeal more.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs S Bury & Ralph Kelly & Odhran O'Neill & Thomas Cox
Spotlight: Has finished second on six occasions during his winless 24-race career; he might drop lucky one day but he's not the percentage call.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: A 9yo with ten wins on the board and arrives here in good form, last time overcoming a slow start to finish second at the Curragh (7f); won't mind returning to further and he's firmly in the mix.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice for James Ferguson last year; finding her feet for new yard and was clear over 1f out before reeled in at Leopardstown (1m, good; Bucaneer's Spirit fourth); well drawn for one who likes to be up there; likely contender.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: Makes handicap debut after an absence and was well beaten in a claimer on final start.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
OwnerName: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan
Spotlight: Record of 3-51 so hard to win with and recent struggles continued here on Thursday when he walked out of the stalls.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: Belatedly off the mark in a course maiden in May but has offered little in subsequent handicaps; exposed now and tries blinkers.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: Limerick winner who was a beaten favourite only two days later at Gowran off this 7lb higher mark and again failed to get involved at Fairyhouse on Wednesday.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: An 8yo mare with five wins from 45 starts; not in great form and another headgear switch.

RaceName: Smiths Of Drogheda Ford Classic Handicap
Spotlight: Minor form, including a 7f AW handicap; 8lb out of the weights on this stable debut.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dam 6.5f-7.5f AW winner (including 2yo/Listed; 103); showed up well for a fair way before fading into sixth on his debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; 66-1) four weeks ago and may come on for that; claims.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam 5.7f winner (RPR 56), half-sister to 5.7f/6f winner Titus Secret, out of useful 5f-7f winner; gelded ahead of his debut and best watched.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam 5f-7f maiden (RPR 54), half-sister to winners Yolo Again (5f including 2yo), Brave Nation (5f including 2yo) and Gunnerside (5f-7f), out of 5f 2yo winner; gelded ahead of his debut and others are preferred.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Caught the eye keeping on into fourth on his debut at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) in March but he's failed to build on that in two starts since, albeit with an excuse at Kempton in May having been caught wide; switches back to turf.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam runner-up 6f AW 2yo/1m turf (RPR 72), out of unraced sister to useful winners Appeared (9.4f-1m4f) and Apparate (1m2f-14.5f), family of dual Arc winner Enable; gelded ahead of his debut and the betting should be informative.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Shaped well in both starts as a 2yo, including when third at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft) in October; found Listed race too much at Newbury (1m2f, good) on reappearance and did too much too soon when second of three in a novice at Nottingham (8.5f, soft) three weeks ago; wore hood and cheekpieces last time, with the latter now removed; the one to beat now dropped back in trip.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Encouraging debut when fourth of ten at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month but she was well beaten at Southwell (6f, AW) two weeks ago; steps back up in trip and returns to turf with better needed.
12-1 and never landed a blow on debut in a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle (22.5f, good) 39 days ago; in good hands but may need more time.

Strong winter maiden hurdle form; disappointed on handicap debut at Fairyhouse in April but runner up returned to maiden company at Cork (24.5f, good) on latest; blinkers replace cheekpieces; rated 117 and sets the standard.

Tailed off on debut at Fairyhouse (2m4f, yld-sft); sent off at just 3-1 upped in trip on better ground at Punchestown (23.5f, gd-yld), but weakened late and beaten 18l into fourth; overlooked by O'Keeffe; needs more.
Always near the back before pulled up on debut at Punchestown when 66-1; cheekpieces go on.
Point-to-point winner (dead-heated) but that form has not worked out; not as good twice since and headgear applied for rules' debut; best watched.
Point-to-point winner at the fifth attempt at Dromahane in April; soundly beaten at long odds in two bumpers and a Sligo maiden hurdle on latest; hard to fancy.

Sent off at 33-1 on debut in a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle (22.5f, good) 39 days ago but belied those odds to run a fine race in second, beaten 6l by a progressive rival; shaped like further would suit that days, so he's a big player upped in trip.
Long odds and no impact in bumpers at Clonmel and at this venue; best watched stepped up markedly in trip for hurdling debut.
Getaway mare is bred to have a future over hurdles, but she was pulled up in both point-to-points; can only watch for now.

Point-to-point winner in October 2024; no impact in a trio of maiden hurdles at 2m-3m in the spring of 2025; modest efforts back in point-to-points when last seen in October/November; others make more appeal.

Best effort in three point-to-points a 13l second at Tullaherin and the winner of that race has since placed in a bumper; pulled up at Toomebridge on latest, though, and is likely best watched on rules' debut.
No signs yet of winning potential in five bumpers and three runs over hurdles; beaten 63l in mares' hurdle at Kilbeggan latest; rated just 79 and hard to make a case for on form.
Triple-figure odds for all three starts in maiden hurdles and ran accordingly; moves up in trip; seems safe to rule out.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Shaped well when third of eight at Ripon (6f, good) on her debut in May; failed to build on it on slower ground at Carlisle last month; not tried in a tongue-tie but needs to improve to trouble the principals.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 22-1 for her recent debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) and showed some promise in finishing a 6l fifth of the 13 runners; can do better but such progress is essential in this field.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: 400,000gns yearling; weak 7-2 shot when a remote third of four at Carlisle (5f, good; first two home useful fillies) in May; can do better.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 400,000euros yearling; sixth foal; closely related to 5f/6f winner Rogue Lightning (including 2yo/Listed/Qatar; RPR 115), half-sister to two winners including Sixtygeesbaby (6f including 2yo; 92); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (84), half-sister to useful 5f/6f winner Pennsylvania Dutch; looks the part on paper.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: She has recorded an RPR in the 50s in finishing fifth in 7f events at Wetherby (fillies' maiden) and Musselburgh (seller); nurseries more suitable after this.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 45,000gns yearling, £100,000 breeze-up 2yo; sixth foal; half-sister to four winners including Spirit Of Nguru (7f AW 2yo; RPR 95), Blue Nguru (5f AW 2yo; 70) and Rastafara (US 1m turf); dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner (98); likely type on paper and trainer took this race in 2019, 2021 and 2023 (all three winners had experience).

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: She has improved with each of her three runs in novice company, most recently finishing second of nine at Sandown (5f, good) three weeks ago; should go well.

OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Well-bred No Nay Never colt was sent off at 6-1 on debut when looked yard's second string at Cork (6f, good) and was beaten 6.5l into fifth; the second and third from that race have won maidens since and the fourth ran well in the Chesham Stakes; dropping back to 5f looks a positive and he's likely to improve plenty for that initial experience.
Long odds for both starts and soundly beaten; second effort a bit better than his first but much more needed; will qualify for nurseries after this.

OwnerName: Giselle De Aguiar & AMO Racing Limited
Spotlight: Yard does well with juveniles but this Perfect Power colt finished well beaten in a pair of Curragh maidens over 5f and 6f on testing ground in April; needs to improve markedly for better ground and a gelding operation.

Modest run on debut at Navan (6f, good) but much better twice since when a 2l third at the Curragh (6f, gd-yld,) and a 5.5l second at Down Royal (5f, soft); the form of that Curragh run was boosted by Sirocco Sands at York last weekend; solid claims.
Tailed off on testing ground at the Curragh on debut (5f); better effort on good ground at Naas (6f) in May when a 9l fifth of 7 behind Confucius; needs to improve again dropped back in trip.

Betrayed inexperience when green on debut at Cork (6f, soft); didn't fare much better on good ground at Fairyhouse on latest; worth a go at 5f and likely capable of better in first-time hood but Tradewinds looks yard's best chance.

Promising introduction for this Cotai Glory filly when a 2.25l second of 8 in a Fillies' maiden at Naas (5f, good) 9 days ago; rider reported she was a little intimidated by the winner 2f out; should be more streetwise now and Keane takes over; big chance.
Magna Grecia filly; E1,000 yearling; dam unraced half-sister to Listed winners Band Practice (5f 2yo) and Nice Name (Italian 1m), out of 5f/6f winner; likely best watched on debut for local yard.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Seven wins include two at this track over 6f (good to soft/good to firm) but her last success came in October 2024; beat only one rival home on her reappearance here over 6f last month and now drops back in trip on her return to classified company.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Often front-runner; won twice last year, including over this C&D on good to firm; disappointed on heavy ground eight months ago and not seen since.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 24-race maiden but he's been runner-up on three occasions and was beaten only a neck when third in a classified event at Brighton (5.5f, good; first-time cheekpieces) last month; place claims.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes
OwnerName: Mrs N Davies And Chris Mason
Spotlight: C&D winner on good to firm last June who has posted two respectable efforts over 5f in handicaps at Bath since returning from a 247-day break; contender now that she drops into a classified.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Struggled in three starts in novice/maiden company in June 2024, including when last of two in a first-time hood (retained) at Leicester; best watched on his return from a lengthy absence.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 14-race maiden who was tailed off at Lingfield (7.5f, good) in May on his first start for 76 days; has posted some fair efforts on the AW over this trip but others are preferred.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Struggled on his first five starts but ran respectably when fifth of 14 at Bath (5f, good) three weeks ago; now drops into a classified.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole win from ten starts came at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January when trained by James Owen; went close at Bath (5.5f, good) in May on his second start for this stable and back to some form when he ran respectably in another handicap there on Wednesday; drops into a classified.
Point-to-point winner and 126-rated hurdler, winning 2m6f Downpatrick maiden and 2m4f Perth novice last year; below form when last seen in October, but respected on chase bow.
Bumper and maiden hurdle win came on very testing ground at Navan in February (2m); didn't count on last month's chase debut at Kilbeggan, but entitled to come on for the experience.
Dual hurdles winner last year at about 2m4f; good bit of chasing experience, notably third in 2m6f Galway beginners' last summer and second in 3m Clonmel novice; fourth in this race last year.

Very useful staying hurdler, winning 2m4f handicap here and 3m contests at Listowel and Leopardstown last term; career-best when last seen finishing third of 24 in 3m Listed handicap at Punchestown festival in April; closely related to yard's good chaser San Salvador, so good chance he'll take to chasing; trip on the short side, but has won around here before so greatly respected.
121-rated hurdler made a promising enough start to chasing when third in 2m7f beginners' here in May, not quite getting home under positive ride; shorter trip here could suit, but more needed.

OwnerName: Mrs N Flynn & John Joseph Flynn
Spotlight: 123-rated hurdler whose best form is on softer ground; unseated on last month's chase debut at Punchestown when leading; can't be ruled out if putting in a clear round.

118-rated hurdler won Bellewstown maiden and Kelso novice late last summer on good ground; none too fluent and pulled up on chasing debut at Kilbeggan in late May, so needs to jump much better; yard going well and has his ground.

Winning hurdler over 2m4f on heavy for Gavin Cromwell last season; pick of five attempts chasing when outstayed late on by a well-handicapped rival in 2m7f Limerick handicap at Christmas (soft-to-heavy); not so good following month at Navan and given a break since; ground likely on the quick side.

Smart hurdler (rated 143) at his best, albeit soundly beaten in competitive handicaps final two starts last term; bit laboured in his jumping on chasing debut at Clonmel in April (soft), but has had time off since and no surprise if he returned with an improved display; has to be considered on favoured ground.
Dual hurdles winner on good ground rated 99; ground to suit, but lots to find with several of these now switched to fences.
Downpatrick handicap hurdle winner last summer is rated 95 in that discipline; absent since October and this looks a stiff task on chasing debut.

RaceName: Belazu, Part Of The William Jackson Food Group Handicap
Spotlight: Began his campaign with a second and first over C&D in April; his progress has continued, adding a win at Redcar (5f, good) 13 days ago; well drawn for a prominent racer and should go well despite his career-high mark.

RaceName: Belazu, Part Of The William Jackson Food Group Handicap
Spotlight: Five wins and a second from nine attempts over C&D; ended 2025 in flying form and returned off a brutal mark as a consequence; handicapper relenting now and her third at Nottingham 13 days ago was a step back in the right direction; this should be run to suit and she's a strong contender.

RaceName: Belazu, Part Of The William Jackson Food Group Handicap
Spotlight: Blows hot and cold but he is on a dangerous mark and his second of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) in May was a fine effort; subsequent run in the Dash at Epsom can be forgiven (ground) and this should be more to his liking; other pace influences on show.

RaceName: Belazu, Part Of The William Jackson Food Group Handicap
OwnerName: Ms Janelle Cao, Ross Harmon & Partner
Spotlight: Added another two AW wins to his tally earlier in the year; unplaced in three runs back on turf but dropping back into a Class 4 should help; others preferred all the same.

RaceName: Belazu, Part Of The William Jackson Food Group Handicap
Spotlight: Running creditably this season and she is closely matched with Elegant Erin on their Nottingham run 13 days ago; suspicion she is vulnerable again here though; cheekpieces replace a visor today.

RaceName: Belazu, Part Of The William Jackson Food Group Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer; on a good mark and his latest Hamilton third offered hope he would be adding to his tally soon; tongue-tie returns today; considered.

RaceName: Belazu, Part Of The William Jackson Food Group Handicap
Spotlight: In good form on AW earlier in the year; unplaced both starts since returning to turf this summer and others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Belazu, Part Of The William Jackson Food Group Handicap
Spotlight: Made all to win a 5f nursery at Thirsk for Bryan Smart in September 2024; soft-ground defeat one month later easy enough to forgive; been gelded since last seen; 630-day absence the obvious negative now starting out for a new yard (same owner).

OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Three second this year include two in 6f handicaps at the Curragh; however, disappointing in last two races (5f maiden/7f handicap) and now 0-17; these terms aren't favourable, either.
300-1 when tailed off in a 7f soft-ground maiden at Limerick two weeks ago.

Consistent RPRs in his first three races before a quiet final run last season; 5-1 for his return in a 5f maiden at Navan where he unseated early after stumbling; should be competitive in this company.
Near misses in two claimers during May extended her losing sequence to 16 so she's had plenty of chances now; rated only 62 so should be vulnerable again.
Beaten six times but two of his runs read well in this company and last time was one of those, finishing second of 13 in a 5f maiden at Cork won by the odds-on favourite; big shout.
0-5 and has yet to post a RPR higher than 68, so likely has quite a lot to find.
2,000euros yearling; dam 5f winner (RPR 82) and half-sister to 7f winner Cill Mocheallog; can only be watched on what is a belated debut; has tongue tied.

Major form claims on her seconds in 6f maidens at the Curragh last season but she's threatening to go the wrong way this year (5f maiden/6f handicap); those two runs were in cheekpieces and now goes in a tongue-tie/blinkers combination.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Showed some ability on his first two starts at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) and Lingfield (7f, good), but he was beaten 20l over 7.5f at the latter track four weeks ago; makes his handicap debut and may be capable of better.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who shaped well at Carlisle (7f, good) last month, rallying for fourth having dropped to the rear after being caught wide, leaving the impression the step up to this trip would suit; shortlisted.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown improved form since returning from a two-month break, winning at Windsor (1m, good to firm) in May and posting two good efforts since, the most recent when finishing second of five six days ago; strong claims if translating that form away from Windsor.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Well held on her first four efforts but she ran a solid race when runner-up to a comfortable winner over this C&D in May (good to firm); that was her first try on ground quicker than good and she has each-way claims.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Opened her account at the seventh attempt when winning a classified event at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) last month and posted another solid effort when fourth at Yarmouth (1m, soft) three weeks ago; returns to handicap company with place claims.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Improved for the fitting of the visor when dead-heating at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) in April and went close again next time; bounced back from a lesser effort on soft ground when a respectable third of nine at Pontefract (1m, good) four days ago and now has the headgear back on (left off last time).

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Well beaten on her debut at Newbury (6f, good to soft) last year and it's been the same story in two starts this season (1m2f/1m); much better needed on her handicap debut and others have stronger claims.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9th-11th October Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who was a length second of nine in first-time cheekpieces at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) in April; struggled in two starts since and now has a bit to prove.
